11.1
FIRST AND SECOND READING
11.1.1
#14839 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 - REZ #25-25 (7211 Greenford Ave)
1.
14839 ZB2026 Amendment No. 1 2026 (7211 Greenford).pdf
Purpose: to amend the previous REZ #18-43 (Bylaw No. 14299), which received Final Adoption on December 16, 2024. This rezoning approved a high-density mixed-use development comprised of a 69-unit, six-storey low-rise market and non-market residential rental building with commercial fronting Kingsway, and a 359-unit, 41-storey strata residential tower with townhouses. The proposed amendment requests approval of a high-density mixed-use development consisting of a six-storey low-rise apartment building with approximately 142 units, and a 27-storey market and non-market mixed-use tower with approximately 276 residential units, inclusive of a two-storey commercial podium fronting Kingsway. Under the transitionary provisions of the Burnaby 2050 OCP adopted by Council in December 2025, the proposal is advancing in accordance with the 1998 OCP, including associated policies.
(Item 7.2., Administrative Report, Council June 23, 2026)
11.2

12.

No items of correspondence were received for Council's decision. All other items of correspondence were published at the Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions package dated July 28, 2026.
13.

14.1
Resolution No. C2026 - 075
THAT
the name "Cameron Community Centre and Library", for the rebuilt facility of the same address, be approved;
THAT
the Library Board be advised of this decision; and
THAT
the approved name be released at the next Open Council meeting.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
15.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: New Burnaby Pay Parking and EV Charging Bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider repealing and replacing the current Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw. A proposed new bylaw would update rules for pay stations and pay parking signage. This could affect where and how you pay for parking and EV charging in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could change how you pay for parking and EV charging in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.1 PROPOSED NEW BURNABY PAY PARKING AND EV CHARGING BYLAW AND RELATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS Purpose: To propose the repeal and replacement of the Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw and bring forward related bylaw amendments to address implementation of pay stations and pay parking signage.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on density bonus reserve fund for ACC program
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a new density bonus statutory reserve fund and temporary internal borrowing to support the amended Amenity Cost Charges (ACC) Program. The ACC program sets fees developers pay to fund community amenities like parks and community centres. If approved, this change would allow the city to borrow internally to cover costs while the new program is set up.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect development costs and amenity funding in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.2 INTERNAL BORROWING IN SUPPORT OF ACC PROGRAM AMENDMENT Purpose: To request that Council approve establishing a density bonus statutory reserve fund and temporary internal borrowing to support the amended Amenity Cost Charges (ACC) Program.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Six-storey market rental rezoning on Sidley Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing a rezoning application (REZ #26-08) for three properties at 5168, 5180, and 5192 Sidley Street. The proposal would allow a six-storey market rental building under the Royal Oak Community Plan. This is a first step — if authorized, the rezoning would go to First and Second Reading at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.3 REZ #26-08 - 5168, 5180 and 5192 SIDLEY STREET - SIX-STOREY MARKET RENTAL (ROYAL OAK COMMUNITY PLAN) Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #26-07 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Temporary Use Permit for parking and works yard at Lougheed Hwy
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving Temporary Use Permit (TUP) #26-04, which would allow a parking facility, transportation facility, and works yard at 6800 Lougheed Highway, 6900 and 6905 Blackbird Boulevard, and 2904 Salmonberry Avenue. A Temporary Use Permit is a short-term permission to use land in a way that is not normally allowed under current zoning rules.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the council meeting or submit written comments to have your say on this proposed permit.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.4 TUP #26-04 - 6800 LOUGHEED HIGHWAY, 6900 AND 6905 BLACKBIRD BOULEVARD AND 2904 SALMONBERRY AVENUE Purpose: To seek Council approval for Temporary Use Permit (TUP) #26-04 to permit a Parking Facility, Transportation Facility, and Works Yard at 6800 Lougheed Highway, 6900 and 6905 Blackbird Boulevard, and 2904 Salmonberry Avenue.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on Irmin and Watling sidewalk contract award
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a contract award for the Irmin and Watling Sidewalk Package project. If approved, this would authorize construction of new sidewalks in those areas. The decision is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.5 CA - (CA-7014) IRMIN AND WATLING SIDEWALK PACKAGE Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the (CA-7014) Irmin and Watling Sidewalk Package project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on Boundary Road repaving contract award
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for the Weather Damage Repaving Program on Boundary Road. This project would repair pavement damaged by weather on a major Burnaby road. If approved, the contract will be awarded to a contractor to carry out the repaving work.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.6 CA - (CA-3338) WEATHER DAMAGE REPAVING PROGRAM - BOUNDARY ROAD Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the (CA-3338) Weather Damage Repaving Program – Boundary Road project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for Kensington Complex roof replacement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for the 2026 Capital Re-Roofing Program at the Kensington Complex Roof A. This is a proposed decision that would approve a contractor and budget for the roof replacement work.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.7 CA - 2026 CAPITAL RE-ROOFING PROGRAM - KENSINGTON COMPLEX ROOF A Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the 2026 Capital Re-Roofing Program – Kensington Complex Roof A

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on server support contract award
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a contract award for the supply and delivery of server product support and subscriptions. This is a routine procurement decision that would authorize the city to purchase ongoing technical support and software subscriptions for its server infrastructure.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.8 CA - SERVER PRODUCT SUPPORT AND SUBSCRIPTIONS Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of server product support and subscriptions.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on Bonsor Recreation Complex roof replacement contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a contract award for replacing Roof A and C at the Bonsor Recreation Complex. This is a proposed decision — if approved, the work would proceed to replace aging roof sections at the facility.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.9 CA - BONSOR RECREATION COMPLEX ROOF A AND C REPLACEMENT Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Bonsor Recreation Complex Roof A and C Replacement.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract award for Lewarne Park playground and park improvements
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a contract for construction of a new playground, multi-sport court upgrades, and related improvements at Lewarne Park. This is a proposed decision — the contract has not yet been awarded.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.10 CA - LEWARNE PARK PLAYGROUND AND PARK IMPROVEMENTS Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Construction of a new playground, multi-sport court upgrades, and related improvements at Lewarne Park.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on contract change for electrical engineering services
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a contract change for electrical engineering services support related to transportation. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.11 CC - ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING SERVICES SUPPORT - TRANSPORTATION Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for Electrical Engineering Services Support – Transportation.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: New licence agreements for five Burnaby child care centres
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing new Licence and Operating Agreements for five City-owned child care centres. If approved, these agreements would set the terms for how the centres are run. The decision is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new agreements could affect your child care arrangement or business operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 8.1 FMC - CITY-OWNED CHILD CARE CENTRES NEW LICENCE AND OPERATING AGREEMENTS Purpose: To seek Council authorization to enter into new Licence and Operating Agreements for five City-owned child care centres.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Capital grant for child care at Brentwood Park school site
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a one-time capital grant from the Community Benefit Bonus Reserve to the Burnaby School District. The grant would fund construction of a child care facility at the Brentwood Park Elementary School site (1455 Delta Avenue). This is a proposed decision — it has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 8.2 FMC - APPROVAL OF CAPITAL GRANT FUNDING FOR CHILD CARE ON SCHOOL SITE Purpose: To provide Council with updated information on provincial funding received, and request approval of a one-time capital grant to the Burnaby School District from the Community Benefit Bonus Reserve for the construction of a child care facility at the Brentwood Park Elementary School site (1455 Delta Avenue).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning for low-rise multi-family with childcare at Wayburne Drive
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing a rezoning application (REZ #25-16) for 3588 Wayburne Drive to allow a low-rise multi-family development with a childcare facility. The proposal is at an early stage — Council is being asked to forward it to First and Second Reading at a future meeting, meaning it has not yet been approved or enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required — monitor future council meetings for public hearing dates.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 8.3 PDC - REZ #25-16 - 3588 WAYBURNE DRIVE - AMENDED LOW-RISE MULTI-FAMILY DEVELOPMENT WITH CHILDCARE FACILITY Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-16 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: YVR Airport zoning regulation changes under review
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive an update on proposed changes to the Vancouver International Airport Zoning Regulations (VIAZR). These regulations control land use and development near the airport to ensure safety and compatibility. The update is informational at this stage — no decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.1 YVR AIRPORT ZONING REGULATIONS UPDATE Purpose: To provide Council with an update on proposed changes to the Vancouver International Airport Zoning Regulations (VIAZR).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby lifeguard training pilot with SD41
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a Lifeguard Training Pilot Program in partnership with School District 41 (SD41). The program aims to train new lifeguards to address staffing shortages. A decision has not yet been made — this item is on the agenda for discussion and a vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.2 LIFEGUARD TRAINING PILOT PROGRAM WITH SD41 Purpose: To inform on the Lifeguard Training Pilot Program with SD41.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to receive June 2026 building permit activity report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from June 1 to June 30, 2026. The report provides information on construction activity in the city. This is an information item only — no bylaw or policy change is proposed.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.3 SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 06 FROM JUNE 1 - JUNE 30, 2026 Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive overview of Burnaby Community Schools services
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive an informational overview of the background, purpose, and funding for Community Schools in Burnaby. This is not a decision item — no bylaw or policy change is proposed at this time.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.4 ECC - OVERVIEW OF COMMUNITY SCHOOLS SERVICES AND PROGRAMMING Purpose: To provide Council with information on the background, purpose, and funding for Community Schools in Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive status update on IT initiatives for April–June 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on IT initiatives covering April to June 2026. This report is for information only and does not propose any new rules or changes that would directly affect businesses.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.5 FMC - SU: IT INITIATIVES APRIL - JUNE 2026 Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) of IT initiatives for April to June 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive update on major Burnaby engineering projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a status update on major engineering projects from May to July 2026. This is an information report, not a decision item, but it may signal upcoming work or changes that could affect local businesses, such as road construction or utility upgrades.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.6 FMC - SU: MAJOR ENGINEERING PROJECTS - MAY 2026 - JULY 2026 Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on major Engineering Projects from May to July 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive status update on significant civic projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a status update on significant civic projects as of July 2026. This is an information report, not a decision item, but it may signal upcoming changes or priorities that could affect local businesses.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.7 FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - JULY 2026 Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on significant civic projects as of July 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to review Burnaby's financial report for Period 05 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a financial report that compares the City's actual spending and revenue for the first five months of 2026 against the 2026–2030 Financial Plan adopted in February. This is a routine update to show whether the City is on track with its budget. No new taxes or fees are proposed in this report.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.8 FMC - FINANCIAL REPORT AS AT 2026 PERIOD 05 Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the City's financial activity as of the end of Period 05, and to compare that activity with the 2026 - 2030 Financial Plan adopted by Council on February 10, 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to receive update on Burnaby's HOME housing strategy
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a progress update on the HOME: Burnaby's Housing and Homelessness Strategy and non-market housing projects on City-owned lands. This update covers actions taken so far under the strategy and the status of affordable housing developments on public land. No final decision is being made at this time — the report is for information and discussion.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.9 PDC - PROGRESS UPDATE: 2025 HOME STRATEGY IMPLEMENTATION AND CITY NON-MARKET HOUSING PROJECTS Purpose: To provide Council with a progress update on actions under HOME: Burnaby’s Housing and Homelessness Strategy and the non-market housing projects on City-owned lands.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to simplify event permits for community producers
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a report on improvements to support community event producers. The proposed changes aim to simplify access to City services, strengthen event safety planning, and enhance the overall customer experience for those organizing events in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.10 PRCC - SUPPORT FOR COMMUNITY EVENT PRODUCERS Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of improvements to support community event producers focused on simplifying access to City services, strengthening event safety planning, and enhancing the overall customer experience.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive update on Learn to Swim Pilot Program
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive an update on the School Learn to Swim Pilot Program. This is a proposed program update, not a final decision. The purpose is to inform Council about the pilot's progress.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 9.11 PRCC - SU: LEARN TO SWIM PILOT PROGRAM Purpose: To provide Council with an update on the School Learn to Swim Pilot Program.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Zoning amendment for 7211 Greenford Ave, Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on first and second reading of a zoning bylaw amendment for 7211 Greenford Ave. The proposed change would replace a previously approved 41-storey tower and 69-unit low-rise with a new plan: a 27-storey mixed-use tower (about 276 units, including market and non-market rental) and a six-storey low-rise apartment (about 142 units), plus a two-storey commercial podium on Kingsway. This proposal is moving forward under the old 1998 Official Community Plan, not the new Burnaby 2050 plan. A public hearing and final vote would follow if first and second readings pass.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and development potential.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body: 11.1.1 #14839 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 - REZ #25-25 (7211 Greenford Ave) Purpose: to amend the previous REZ #18-43 (Bylaw No. 14299), which received Final Adoption on December 16, 2024. This rezoning approved a high-density mixed-use development comprised of a 69-unit, six-storey low-rise market and non-market residential rental building with commercial fronting Kingsway, and a 359-unit, 41-storey strata residential tower with townhouses. The proposed amendment requests approval of a high-density mixed-use development consisting of a six-storey low-rise apartment building with approximately 142 units, and a 27-storey market and non-market mixed-use tower with approximately 276 residential units, inclusive of a two-storey commercial podium fronting Kingsway. Under the transitionary provisions of the Burnaby 2050 OCP adopted by Council in December 2025, the proposal is advancing in accordance with the 1998 OCP, including associated policies.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Density Bonus Reserve Fund Bylaw up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw to establish a Density Bonus Reserve Fund. This fund would collect money from developers who get extra density (permission to build more floor space) in exchange for community benefits like parks, affordable housing, or transit improvements. The bylaw is up for first, second, and third reading — meaning council is set to vote on whether to create the fund. If approved, the fund would set rules for how these developer payments are collected and spent.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect development costs and community amenity negotiations in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 11.2.1 #14840 - Burnaby Density Bonus Reserve Fund Bylaw Purpose: to establish the Density Bonus Reserve Fund

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Pay Parking and EV Charging Bylaw 2026 up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow pay parking and electric vehicle charging on City lands in Burnaby. If approved, the bylaw would permit the City to charge fees for parking and provide EV charging services on municipal property. This is a first, second, and third reading — meaning council is considering adopting the bylaw, but it has not yet been enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect where and how you park or charge EVs on City land.
agenda_briefs.industries: Retail, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body: 11.2.2 #14842 - Burnaby Pay Parking and Electric Vehicle Charging Bylaw 2026 Purpose: to allow pay parking and electric vehicle charging on City lands

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment on pay parking and EV charging fees
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on an amendment to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw that would update fees for pay parking and electric vehicle (EV) charging. The proposed bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026, is up for first, second, and third reading — meaning council is considering approving it in principle. If passed, the new fee schedule would take effect at a later date. Business owners who operate parking lots or EV charging stations should watch for the final decision.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor council's decision — if passed, review the new fee schedule to adjust your parking or EV charging rates accordingly.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Transportation & Logistics
agenda_briefs.source_body: 11.2.3 #14843 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026 Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (pay parking and EV charging)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment on pay parking and EV charging enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009. The amendment would update how the city enforces rules related to pay parking and electric vehicle (EV) charging. If approved, this could change how parking and EV charging violations are ticketed and enforced in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how parking and EV charging rules are enforced in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics
agenda_briefs.source_body: 11.2.4 #14844 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (pay parking and EV charging)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 26-storey tower with rental and strata units at 5852 Patterson Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on third reading of a zoning bylaw amendment that would allow a 26-storey high-rise development at 5852 Patterson Avenue. The project includes strata townhouses, non-market rental apartments, and strata apartments. If approved, this would change the zoning rules for that property to permit the mixed-use building.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect nearby property values and development opportunities.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 11.3.1 #14458 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026 - REZ #17-35 (5852 Patterson Avenue) Purpose: to permit the development of a single 26-storey high-rise multiple family development which includes strata townhouse units, non-market rental apartments, and strata apartments

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Amenity Cost Charges Bylaw amendment up for final adoption
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of an amendment to the Burnaby Amenity Cost Charges Bylaw 2024. The proposed changes would update definitions and Schedule B of the bylaw. Amenity cost charges are fees the city collects from new development to help pay for community amenities like parks, community centres, and libraries. If adopted, this amendment would change how those charges are calculated or applied.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect development costs for new projects in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 11.4.1 #14837 - Burnaby Amenity Cost Charges Bylaw 2024, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 Purpose: to amend Burnaby Amenity Cost Charges Bylaw 2024 (definitions, Schedule B)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Housing agreement for non-market rental at 6887 Palm Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would authorize a Housing Agreement for a non-market rental housing development at 6887 Palm Avenue. If approved, this agreement would lock in the property as affordable rental housing, ensuring it remains non-market for the long term.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 11.4.2 #14841 - Burnaby Housing Agreement (6887 Palm Avenue) Bylaw 2026 Purpose: to authorize the execution of a Housing Agreement for the non-market rental housing development at 6887 Palm Avenue

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council approves name 'Cameron Community Centre and Library' for rebuilt facility
agenda_briefs.summary: Council has unanimously approved the name 'Cameron Community Centre and Library' for the rebuilt facility at the same address. The decision was made in a closed meeting and will be released at the next open council meeting. This is a final decision, not a proposal still under consideration.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 14.1 Resolution No. C2026 - 075 THAT the name "Cameron Community Centre and Library", for the rebuilt facility of the same address, be approved; THAT the Library Board be advised of this decision; and THAT the approved name be released at the next Open Council meeting. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
